document: Accountability and governance _ ICO (United Kingdom - Row 32) (PROVISIONAL)

sections.heading: Take our website user survey
sections.title: Website user survey invitation for feedback
sections.summary: This section invites website users to complete a short survey about website improvements. The survey offers three response options: immediate participation, deferred action, or no interest. It does not establish any legal obligations but provides context about the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025's commencement and the ICO's role in data protection guidance.

requirements.text: Take our website user survey
We're making improvements to our website and we'd love to hear your thoughts.
Please take five minutes to complete this survey to give your feedback.
Yes I'll take the survey
I'll do this later
I'm not interested

sections.source_quote:
Take our website user survey
We're making improvements to our website and we'd love to hear your thoughts.
Please take five minutes to complete this survey to give your feedback.

sections.heading: The guidance on this page is suitable for large businesses in the public, private and third sectors.
sections.title: Accountability and governance guidance for large businesses
sections.summary: This section outlines guidance for large businesses across public, private, and third sectors on accountability and governance, including contracts, data protection by design and default, data protection impact assessments, data protection officers, and certification schemes. It specifies that small businesses should use separate resources and provides links to detailed guidance on documentation, DPIAs, codes of conduct, and certification schemes.

requirements.text: The guidance on this page is suitable for large businesses in the public, private and third sectors.
Small businesses should use the resources on our small business web hub.
Guide to accountability and governance
Contracts, documentation, data protection by design and default, data protection impact assessments and data protection officers.
Do we need a UK representative?
Guidance discussing receiving personal information from the European Economic Area (EEA).
Contracts and liabilities between controllers and processors
When contracts are needed and why they are important, what needs to be included and responsibility and liability.
Documentation
What is documentation and who needs to document processing activities, what needs to be documented under article 30 and how to document processing activities.
Data protection impact assessments (DPIA)
What is a DPIA, when and how to conduct one, consulting the ICO and examples of 'likely to result in high risk'.
Codes of conduct
Data protection codes of conduct address sector specific data protection issues and support compliance with the UK GDPR'.
We have detailed guidance and other resources for trade organisations and similar representative bodies who may create codes of conduct for their members.
Certification schemes
Data protection certification can help demonstrate data protection in a practical way to businesses, individuals and regulators.
We have detailed guidance and other resources about certifications schemes for organisations.

sections.source_quote: The guidance on this page is suitable for large businesses in the public, private and third sectors.

sections.heading: y
sections.title: Sample DPIA template and feedback options
sections.summary: This section provides a sample Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) template and includes a feedback mechanism for users to indicate whether the page was helpful. It does not contain substantive legal requirements but serves as a resource for users to complete a DPIA and provide input on website usability.

requirements.text: y
